name: Sidrat al-Muntaha
description: Sidrat al-Muntaha, also known as the Lote Tree of the Utmost Boundary, is a sacred tree located in the highest heavens in Islamic cosmology. It marks the end of all heavenly knowledge accessible to prophets and angels, signifying a boundary beyond which no created being can pass. It is described as a majestic tree adorned with divine light and surrounded by angels, representing the ultimate limit of spiritual ascent and divine knowledge. The tree is mentioned in the Quran (Surah An-Najm, 53:14) in connection with the Mi'raj, the Prophet Muhammad's ascent to heaven. During the Mi'raj, the Prophet Muhammad reached Sidrat al-Muntaha and witnessed extraordinary signs of God's presence. The tree symbolizes the limits of human understanding and the vastness of the divine realm, serving as a reminder of the power and majesty of Allah. It is a significant symbol of divine revelation and the ultimate destination of spiritual journeys, marking the boundary between the known and the unknowable.
subtitle: The Lote Tree of Utmost Boundary

keyMyths.title: The Mi'raj
keyMyths.description: During the Mi'raj, Prophet Muhammad reached Sidrat al-Muntaha accompanied by the angel Gabriel. He witnessed the tree's majesty and received divine revelations.

keyMyths.title: Gabriel's Limit
keyMyths.description: Gabriel, the archangel, stated that he could not pass beyond Sidrat al-Muntaha, highlighting the tree as the ultimate boundary even for angels.

sources.author: Various Scholars
sources.description: Interpretations and commentaries on Surah An-Najm, which mentions Sidrat al-Muntaha.
